task_completion.description: Open the 'world_survey' project in the GIS application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIS application (e.g., QField) is open, showing the map canvas and the 'world_survey' project loaded, with layers like 'world_capitals' or 'field_observations' visible in the layer panel or menu.

task_completion.description: Identify the location of Paris on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map is centered on or zoomed into Paris, France (approximately Longitude 2.35, Latitude 48.85). The agent may have tapped on a point in the 'world_capitals' layer to reveal its attributes and confirm it is Paris.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the calculated hazard zone location (45 km Due West of Paris).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map crosshairs or view is centered approximately 45 km west of Paris (around Longitude 1.73, Latitude 48.85). The agent may be using a measuring tool showing a distance of ~45 km and a bearing of ~270 degrees from Paris.

task_completion.description: Start creating a new point feature in the 'field_observations' layer at the target location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app is in editing/digitizing mode for the 'field_observations' layer. The map crosshair is positioned at the target location (West of Paris), and the agent has tapped the button to add/confirm the new feature (usually a green checkmark or plus icon).

task_completion.description: Attempt to enter the required attributes ('Hazard Zone Alpha' and '45km West of Paris') for the new point.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An attribute entry form is visible after placing the point. The agent has typed 'Hazard Zone Alpha' and '45km West of Paris' into the available fields. (Note: If specific 'Name' and 'Notes' fields are missing, accept the agent entering these into any available text fields or saving the point after realizing the fields are absent).

integrity.description: The agent used the GIS application's graphical interface to navigate and create the point.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the mobile GIS app's map interface, layer panels, and digitizing tools, rather than a terminal, script editor, or Python console.

integrity.description: The agent actively navigated the map or used measurement tools to find the offset location.
integrity.visual_evidence: Use of the map canvas, panning, zooming, or the measure tool is visible in the trajectory, showing a genuine attempt to find 45km West of Paris.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a SQLite browser or terminal to directly insert the record into the GeoPackage.
integrity.visual_evidence: The point creation process is done entirely within the GIS app's digitizing interface, with no terminal windows or database management apps shown.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target GeoPackage: 'world_survey.gpkg'. Paris is located at approximately Longitude 2.3488, Latitude 48.8534. The target hazard zone (45 km Due West, 270 degrees) is approximately at Longitude 1.7338, Latitude 48.8534. Note: The 'field_observations' layer fundamentally lacks specific 'Name' and 'Notes' columns in its schema. The agent should be awarded full points for the attribute step if they successfully place the point and attempt to input 'Hazard Zone Alpha' and '45km West of Paris' into any available fields, or if they save the point after discovering the requested fields do not exist.
